evals.name: vague-feature-dump-refuses
evals.prompt: I want to build an AI tool for restaurants. It'll have a dashboard, inventory tracking, maybe some kind of chatbot for staff, and it'll use AI to predict stuff. Can you turn this into an idea brief?
evals.expected_output: Content-gate failure. Skill emits the tight blocked-response (verdict + per-gate what's-missing/why/question), refuses to emit a brief, does NOT run verification or write a build seed. Blocked-response is shorter than a brief would be.

assertions.text: Refuses to emit a full brief (no tagged field set, no Build seed)
Identifies the input as a feature-list, not a crisp problem
Flags the missing specific/reachable user gate
Blocked-response is tight: one-line verdict + per-gate question, no pre-written brief
Does not run dynamic verification on un-gated input

evals.name: solid-dump-emits-clean-build-carrying-brief
evals.prompt: Idea brief please. Estonian bookkeepers waste ~3 hours a month manually reconciling bank statements against invoices in their accounting software because the bank export format doesn't match. I'm a bookkeeper, I do this myself, and I've watched 4 colleagues do the same workaround with a messy spreadsheet. Target user: solo and small-firm bookkeepers in Estonia, ~2000 of them. They'd pay maybe 20 EUR/month. The risky part is whether they'd trust an automated match over their own eyes.
evals.expected_output: Passes the three gates and emits a tagged brief in the template shape. Includes a Build seed (core capability, key inputs/integrations, hard constraints, thinnest first slice). bookkeeper-watched-colleagues = [fact]; price + trust = [assumption]; riskiest assumption = trust in automated matching. Output is lint-clean: every field one sentence + tag, no caveat-essay, verification log <=5 lines.

assertions.text: Emits a brief (gates pass), not a blocked-response
Riskiest-assumption line is one sentence + risk-type + one 'if wrong' clause, directly under the title
Build seed present with all four lines (core capability, key inputs/integrations, hard constraints, thinnest first slice)
Every claim line ends with [fact]/[assumption] and a provenance word
watched-4-colleagues marked [fact]; 20 EUR price and trust concern marked [assumption]
Riskiest assumption is the trust-in-automated-matching concern

Output is lint-clean (intelligent model): no 'Note:'/'Bottom line'/reframing essay, no marketing adjectives, every field concrete and non-vague (no TBD/it-depends) — judged on definiteness, NOT word/line counts
On a clean pass the brief is written to ./spec/brief.md (file-only) using the verbatim field anchors from the shared handoff doc

evals.name: solution-in-disguise-reframes
evals.prompt: Make me an idea brief. The idea is a mobile app with a big red button that lets people report workplace safety incidents. For construction companies.
evals.expected_output: Applies the disguised-solution test ('big red button' is a solution), reframes to the underlying need (fast/low-friction on-site incident capture), and either blocks for the missing specific user/buyer or interrogates it. Does not transcribe the solution as the problem. If it emits, the build seed and tags are present and lint-clean.

assertions.text: Applies the disguised-solution test to the 'big red button' framing
Reframes to the underlying need (low-friction on-site incident capture), not the UI
Asks for / names the specific user and buyer (separates them if different)
Does not record the solution verbatim as the problem
Any emitted brief is lint-clean and carries a Build seed
